name: legba
title: legba

arguments.description: Specify a single OID to read, if not specified the entire SNMP tree is walked
arguments.raw: --snmp-oid <SNMP_OID>  Specify a single OID to read, if not specified the entire SNMP tree is walked

arguments.description: Specify a maximum number of OIDs to walk. Set to 0 to walk the entire SNMP tree (it may take a long time) [default: 25]
arguments.raw: --snmp-max <SNMP_MAX>  Specify a maximum number of OIDs to walk. Set to 0 to walk the entire SNMP tree (it may take a long time) [default: 25]

arguments.description: Remote address to test the proxying for [default: ifcfg.co]
arguments.raw: --socks5-address <SOCKS5_ADDRESS>  Remote address to test the proxying for [default: ifcfg.co]

arguments.description: Remote port to test the proxying for [default: 80]
arguments.raw: --socks5-port <SOCKS5_PORT>        Remote port to test the proxying for [default: 80]

arguments.description: Use a NTLM hash instead of a password
arguments.raw: --rdp-ntlm                 Use a NTLM hash instead of a password

arguments.description: AutoLogon mode in case of SSL negotiation
arguments.raw: --rdp-auto-logon           AutoLogon mode in case of SSL negotiation

arguments.description: Use v5 of the MQTT protocol
arguments.raw: --mqtt-v5   Use v5 of the MQTT protocol

arguments.description: Use SSL/TLS connection (mqtts://) with certificate verification disabled
arguments.raw: --mqtt-ssl  Use SSL/TLS connection (mqtts://) with certificate verification disabled

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=16e4f2ea143e1532c7c1736124cf1ffafab7ec82, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Multiprotocol credentials bruteforcer / password sprayer and enumerator
Legba is a multiprotocol credentials bruteforcer / password sprayer and
enumerator built with Rust and the Tokio asynchronous runtime in order to
achieve better performances and stability while consuming less resources than
similar tools.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use legba because ### legba.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `legba [OPTIONS] [PLUGIN]` as documented upstream for legba.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair legba with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `legba --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### legba.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install legba
Install with: sudo apt install legba
